channel.title: Silvier Linings
channel.description: Recent blog posts on Silvier Linings

item.title: Bricks of Chicago Quilt
item.description: I love the idea and execution of this quilt inspired by different Chicago brick patterns. The artist also has some beautiful prints for sale on etsy. View this post on Instagram A post shared by Liz Barr (@lizbarrrr)

item.title: Parenting Haikus, 4/20/26-4/26/26
item.description: A blissful four days With good strong long naps for him Me alive again * And then just of course Disruption, mayhem, madness Fifteen minute naps * He has been cleared of Telescoping intestines And hip dysplasia * Music class was fun! I might be the only one singing. Babies coo. * He got his two month shots One Band-Aid on each leg Screamed quite loud, then slept

item.title: Eating Pooping Doll
item.description: Hand-sewn using old socks, based on design by Kate Donahue. Completed around 2009.

item.title: Expert Difference Spotters
item.description: Handmade &ldquo;medals&rdquo; made as gifts for friends who completed our spot the difference holiday cards in 2024.

item.title: Finger Puppets
item.description: Handmade embroidery of monster finger puppets.

item.title: Kale E-Tegami
item.description: E-Tegami postcard made at 61st Street Farmer&rsquo;s Market, using watercolor pencil and Sharpie.

item.title: My Bouquet
item.description: Handmade embroidery of a bouquet of eyeballs and nipples.

item.title: Sweater No. 18
item.description: My first completed knitted sweater! Sweater No. 18 pattern by My Favourite Things.

item.title: Winter Moon Quilt
item.description: Self-designed quilt. Hand sewn and quilted. Inspired by and drawing on instructions from Heidi Parkes.

item.title: About
item.description: Hi, I&rsquo;m Silvie Senauke. I like to make weird little things, like this blog. Check out the Craft Museum to see some of the other things I&rsquo;ve made. I live in Chicago with my husband and son. This site is part of my personal maternity leave agenda — a way to feel connected to creating and my own identity while caring for a tiny helpless being! I&rsquo;m taking inspiration from a couple of sites I enjoy most: kottke.org (short posts, a window into the strange interesting random parts of the world); Cup of Jo (style, highlights of objects she loves, parenting); The Strategist (things I might want to buy, product reviews).
